name: Apollo A7LB
tagline: The J-mission suit — added a waist joint so astronauts could sit in the Lunar Rover
description: A7L derivative for the extended J-class lunar missions. The signature change was a waist-joint convolution that allowed astronauts to bend forward and sit in the Lunar Roving Vehicle — without it, the J-mission EVAs of Apollo 15, 16, and 17 would not have been possible. Same PLSS, OPS, and 3.7 psi pressure as A7L; same gold visor. Last flown on Apollo 17 in December 1972.
best_known_for: The J-mission suit (Apollo 15, 16, 17) — added a waist convolution that let astronauts sit in the Lunar Roving Vehicle
